summary: Stancil argues that BI and visualization are far more complex than feature backlogs suggest, so “thin” consumption apps coordinated by a data OS are unlikely to deliver a coherent user experience. He proposes a middle path: a strong, general-purpose BI “planet” for exploration, surrounded by specialized satellites for specific use cases.
money_quotes: We should ship an experience that mirrors what people want to do, not one that mirrors the org charts of the teams helping them do it.
For this reason, I don’t think it’s feasible to make BI thin.
Visualization on its own is a very heavy piece of technology, and that’s only a fraction of what BI tools need to do.
We need a global exploration tool that works for most general uses, surrounded by satellites that solve specific problems really well.
key_insight: Because visualization and BI are inherently complex experience layers, a single general-purpose “planet” tool with specialized satellites is more realistic than a fully decentralized ecosystem of thin apps.
